skill.name: ascii-preview-generate
skill.description: Creates ASCII text previews of PDF page layouts from page images and rich text extraction data for downstream HTML generation.
skill.summary: Generate readable ASCII previews that show PDF page structure, text hierarchy, and layout relationships.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown formatting and ordinary prose, with no executable code, shell commands, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, or reconnaissance behavior. The skill remains medium risk because it directs agents to read and write local artifacts and send document content to Claude for analysis.

medium_findings.title: Document Content Sent to External AI Service
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to send a PNG visual reference and extracted JSON text data to Claude. This is legitimate for the workflow, but it can disclose sensitive PDF contents if users process private documents without approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly say to send the PNG image to Claude and include extracted JSON data. No malicious endpoint is present, so the concern is controlled data exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Flexible Local File Read and Write Scope
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts input and output paths for extraction JSON, page images, and preview files. This is expected for the workflow, but broad paths can cause accidental reads or overwrites if callers do not constrain artifact locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access is clearly described and appears necessary for the skill. The risk depends on user-controlled path handling by the calling agent, which is not implemented in this skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences or inline code formatting for file names and examples. No Ruby code, shell command, command interpolation, or executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is Markdown prose, inline code, or a fenced example block. I found no executable source file or command invocation associated with these backticks.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match ordinary prose such as description, provides, destroyed, and Design Notes. No hash function, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language documentation, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any other weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections occur in sample textbook content, UTF-8 validation guidance, and AI response handling guidance. No system probing, network scanning, host discovery, or environment enumeration is instructed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is documentation and validation prose. I found no commands or instructions that enumerate networks, hosts, processes, users, or system configuration.

content.value_statement: PDF layout is hard to inspect from extracted text alone. This skill creates readable ASCII previews that combine page images and extraction metadata.
actual_capabilities: Reads a page PNG and rich extraction JSON when provided.
Prompts Claude to compare visual layout with extracted text metadata.
Produces annotated ASCII previews with page boundaries and element labels.
Summarizes headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, images, fonts, and confidence.
Saves the preview to the page artifact output path.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or a parser implementation.
Quality depends on image clarity and extraction metadata completeness.
May miss complex layouts, rotated text, overlapping elements, or decorative details.
Requires review before using previews as a source for final HTML.

use_cases.title: Review Document Conversion Structure
use_cases.description: Preview the structure of a PDF page before generating semantic HTML from extracted page artifacts.

use_cases.title: Audit Page Extraction Quality
use_cases.description: Compare headings, lists, tables, and paragraphs against the source page during conversion checks.

use_cases.title: Plan Content Migration Work
use_cases.description: Create a readable page summary that helps teams understand layout complexity before migration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Preview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one page artifact set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an ASCII layout preview for this PDF page using the page image and rich extraction JSON. Save it to the specified page artifact folder.

prompt_templates.title: Check Structure Against Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the ASCII preview and compare its element counts with the extraction data. Note any missing headings, lists, tables, or paragraphs.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare HTML Generation Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an ASCII preview optimized for downstream HTML generation. Emphasize reading order, hierarchy, spacing, and reusable semantic sections.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Incomplete Page Inputs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced recovery when one input is missing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the best possible ASCII preview from the available page artifacts. Clearly note whether the image or extraction data was unavailable.

output_examples.input: A textbook page image with rich extraction data for headings, paragraphs, and a bullet list.
output_examples.output: A readable page preview with a page boundary, labeled heading levels, paragraph blocks, bullet list structure, and summary counts.

output_examples.input: A page artifact folder that contains extracted text metadata but no usable page image.
output_examples.output: A text-based layout estimate that notes the missing visual reference and relies on extraction metadata for structure.

output_examples.input: A conversion pipeline page that will later be transformed into HTML.
output_examples.output: A structural preview that highlights reading order, major sections, table presence, image presence, and confidence notes.

best_practices: Provide both the page PNG and rich extraction JSON whenever possible.
Compare summary counts with extraction data before downstream HTML generation.
Keep output files inside the intended page artifact directory.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the ASCII preview as the final source of truth.
Do not process sensitive PDFs unless external model analysis is approved.
Do not use broad output paths that could overwrite unrelated files.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates an ASCII text preview that represents the structure and layout of a PDF page.

faq.question: Does it convert PDFs directly to HTML?
faq.answer: No. It creates an intermediate structural preview used by a later HTML generation workflow.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use both a page PNG and a rich extraction JSON file for the most accurate preview.

faq.question: Can it work without an image?
faq.answer: Yes. It can fall back to extraction data, but the layout may be less accurate.

faq.question: Is the output deterministic?
faq.answer: No. The skill relies on AI analysis, so results may vary between runs.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing output?
faq.answer: Users should verify text accuracy, heading hierarchy, element counts, and reading order against the source page.
